sourceMovie.title: The Exorcism of Emily Rose
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Exorcism of Emily Rose for the same reason it broke through in 2005: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Scott Derrickson keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The crime and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Lord of War
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Exorcism of Emily Rose clicked for you, Lord of War (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Exorcism of Emily Rose?
faq.answer: Try Lord of War, Primal Fear, Deliver Us from Evil—each shares DNA with The Exorcism of Emily Rose while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Exorcism of Emily Rose?
faq.answer: The Exorcism of Emily Rose (2005) is best described as Crime, Drama, Horror, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Exorcism of Emily Rose worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
